text: Increased respiratory difficulty accompanied by a weak, ineffective cough, wheezing, high-pitched crowing noises, and cyanosis are signs of:
explanation: The signs described—difficulty breathing, weak cough, wheezing, stridor, and cyanosis—are classic indicators of a severe airway obstruction, which impedes airflow to the lungs. While these symptoms can occur in other conditions, they most directly point to a compromised airway.
